entries.title: The Trump administration ratcheted up its defense of prediction markets on Thursday, with a federal regulator suing the state of Illinois and its governor
President Trump said he would sign an executive order to pay all Department of Homeland Security employees
President Trump has used tariffs to remake U.S. trade policy, moving from a model of cooperative trade agreements to one that relies on overt economic coercion
Former FTC Chair Lina Khan will launch an economic-policy center at Columbia University offering her style of antitrust-enforcement and economic policy to law students and progressive policymakers
Trump endorsed a two-part plan to quickly fund most of the Department of Homeland Security and then use a special procedure to pay for immigration enforcement
The Democrats are cursing so much lately it feels intentional. Really intentional. Too intentional? Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:

President Trump signed an executive order Tuesday for his administration to create lists of people it considers eligible to vote in federal elections, as well as placing new restrictions on mail-in voting
A federal judge in Washington, D.C. on Tuesday blocked construction of President Trump’s White House ballroom
A federal judge in Texas rejected the Trump administration’s attempt to allow religious leaders to endorse candidates from the pulpit
The Trump administration can’t end federal funding for public media via an executive order, a federal judge ruled Tuesday
Washington State Adopts New Tax on Incomes Over $1 Million
Retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman is a Democrat running for Senate in solidly Republican Florida
President Trump released mock-up images of a gleaming glass tower emblazoned with his last name that will serve as his presidential library once he leaves office
Missouri Republican Rep. Sam Graves, chairman of the House Committee on Transportation, is pulling his plans to seek re-election
President Trump has told associates in recent days that he wants to avoid a protracted war in Iran and that he hopes to bring the conflict to an end in the coming weeks
Democrat Emily Gregory beat a Trump-backed Republican in a special election for the Florida state house district that includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate
President Trump sought to reassure skeptical Americans that the war in Iran is in the national interest, arguing that the operation was necessary to decimate a regime threatening the U.S.
The drone having its moment in the Middle East conflict was designed by the U.S. military itself, using reverse-engineered Iranian technology
President Trump is weighing a military operation to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of uranium from Iran, a complex mission that would likely put U.S. forces inside the country for days or longer

Investigators searched the office of a FEMA contractor as they investigate a network of aides put in place by former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski, according to people familiar with the matter
If President Trump gives the go-ahead, the U.S. could soon have over 17,000 troops on Iran’s doorstep, but any operation to seize an island or territory would be considered complex and dangerous
